heading: Job Signal Lab
purposeLine: A personal lab I use to analyse job listings that may be relevant to me and draw insights from what those roles ask for.
description: I built this lab end-to-end: private corpus intake, ontology matching, embedding clusters, and a published pulse of market signals. What you see here is the results surface, not a job board or career advice.
emptyState: No published pulse yet. The lab is ready; the next corpus recompute will populate this view.

craft.heading: How this is produced
craft.intro: I built this pipeline end-to-end so the published pulse is reproducible craft, not hand-picked charts. Here is the path from a private listing to published aggregates, with plain language first and a short technical note under each stage.

stages.title: Collect Job Listings from the Web
stages.inShort: I collect job listings into a private corpus I control, never a public scrape dump.
stages.underTheHood: Markdown with YAML frontmatter lands in private storage via upload or career-page parse-and-review; ingest refreshes corpus metadata without starting a full recompute.

stages.title: Tag Each Role with Context
stages.inShort: I tag each role with seniority, role family, and optional employer context so later filters mean something.
stages.underTheHood: Controlled vocabularies and an employer registry live in the owner console. Archiving excludes a document from the next recompute without deleting the stored file.

stages.title: Match Technologies Across Listings
stages.inShort: Technology counts come from a curated FinServ/AI dictionary, not every buzzword in the text.
stages.underTheHood: Alias-aware ontology matching collapses variants (for example k8s to Kubernetes) and ignores terms outside the dictionary, so frequency bars stay signal rather than noise.

stages.title: Fingerprint Requirements into Themes
stages.inShort: Each listing gets a numeric fingerprint; similar fingerprints group into themes of related requirements.
stages.underTheHood: Bedrock embeddings of the markdown body, cached by content hash, feed k-means themes. Labels default to top matched technologies; I can override them from the console.

stages.title: Lay Out the Theme Map
stages.inShort: The theme map is a similarity layout where nearby dots share requirement language, not geography or employer.
stages.underTheHood: PCA reduces embeddings to two principal components in the analyse worker. Those coordinates ship with the snapshot so the public map does not invent positions in the browser.

stages.title: Publish Counts, Themes, and the Map
stages.inShort: The page shows published counts, themes, and the map; sensitive listing details stay redacted.
stages.underTheHood: Publication sanitises owner-only fields before the public query. I trigger a recompute from the console when the corpus changes; only one run may be in flight at a time.

skillsCaption: Curated tools and platforms matched in the listings from a controlled ontology, ranked by how often they appear.
clustersCaption: Groups of listings with similar overall requirements. Bars show theme size; the map places documents by how alike their requirement language is.

admin.heading: Corpus recompute
admin.description: Start an analysis run against the active corpus. Only one run may be queued or in progress at a time.
admin.startedMessageWithRunsHint: Recompute started. Run id: {runId}. Open Runs for live status.
admin.secondaryStartDescription: Secondary control for power users. Prefer starting recomputes from Analytics.

corpusAdmin.heading: Corpus management
corpusAdmin.description: Soft-archive active job descriptions to exclude them from the next recompute, or restore archived ones. Objects in storage are not deleted.

upload.heading: Upload job description
upload.description: Add a markdown file with YAML frontmatter. Frontmatter must include a valid collectedAt timestamp. Upload writes to the private raw corpus; ingest runs automatically. Recompute is not started.
upload.uploadedMessage: Uploaded to {s3Key}. Ingest will update corpus metadata shortly.
upload.noFileSelected: Choose a markdown file to upload.

hitlQueue.heading: Intake
hitlQueue.description: Paste a career-page link, parse it, review the result, then submit to the corpus.
hitlQueue.errorLabel: The intake queue is temporarily unavailable.
hitlQueue.emptyReview: Parse a listing to review it here.
hitlQueue.employerUnsetWarning: No employer linked. Create one in Corpus → Employers if needed.
hitlQueue.employerUnknownWarning: Employer id '{id}' is not in the registry.

parseListing.heading: Listing URL
parseListing.description: Employer career or ATS pages work best. Some sites block automated fetch, so paste the page text instead.
parseListing.successMessage: Parsed '{title}'. Adjust fields on the right, then submit.
parseListing.pasteFallbackDescription: Open the listing in your browser, copy the job text (or page HTML), and paste it here. A URL is optional for provenance.

console.heading: Job market owner console
console.description: Manage the private corpus, start recomputes, and review analysis run history. Guests cannot access this console.
console.unauthenticatedDescription: Sign in to open the job market owner console.

items.label: Overview
items.description: Status, queue depth, and shortcuts into each work area.

items.label: Corpus
items.description: Upload job descriptions, employers, metadata, and archive.

items.label: Intake
items.description: Paste a link, parse, review, and submit to the corpus.

items.label: CV and fit
items.description: Edit the canonical CV and compare against roles or market demand.

items.label: Analytics
items.description: Coverage insights, pay and prestige, and start a corpus recompute.

items.label: Runs
items.description: Audit analysis run history, costs, and failures.

overview.heading: Overview
overview.description: See what needs attention, then jump into corpus, review, fit, analytics, or runs.

console.runsDescription: Audit recent recompute lifecycle, document counts, estimated cost, and failures.
console.runsError: Analysis run history is temporarily unavailable.

analyticsWorkspace.heading: Analytics
analyticsWorkspace.description: Coverage and pay insights for the active corpus. Start a recompute here; review run history on Runs.
analyticsWorkspace.startRecomputeDescription: Primary control to start an analysis run against the active corpus.
analyticsWorkspace.secondaryToolsDescription: Theme label overrides and pulse filters apply on the next recompute and publication.

fitWorkspace.heading: CV and fit
fitWorkspace.description: Keep the canonical CV visible while comparing against a role or market demand.
fitWorkspace.saveCvFirstHint: Save a canonical CV before running a comparison.

cv.heading: Canonical CV
cv.description: Private owner CV for later fit comparison against job descriptions and market signals. Never published to guests.
cv.emptyHint: No canonical CV saved yet. Seed from your About profile or write one below.
cv.seededMessage: Seeded from your About profile. Review and save when ready.

compare.heading: CV to job description comparison
compare.description: Compare your canonical CV against one job description from the private corpus. Results stay owner-only and use the FinServ/AI technology ontology.
compare.noCvError: Save a canonical CV before running a comparison.
compare.noJdSelectedError: Choose a job description to compare.
compare.missingMarkdownError: Could not load markdown for the selected job description.
compare.matchesEmpty: No shared ontology technologies found between your CV and this job description.
compare.gapsEmpty: No technology gaps: your CV covers every ontology term in this job description.
compare.matchTalkingPointTemplate: Prepare a concise example of your {technology} work.
compare.gapTalkingPointTemplate: The role emphasises {technology}; explain adjacent experience or your learning plan.
compare.gapLearningTargetTemplate: Build demonstrable {technology} experience for similar roles.

marketCompare.heading: CV to market comparison
marketCompare.description: Compare your canonical CV against published market demand themes and technologies from the latest snapshot. Results stay owner-only and use the trusted ontology and theme labels.
marketCompare.noCvError: Save a canonical CV before running a market comparison.
marketCompare.noPulseError: No published market pulse is available yet. Start a recompute after the corpus has active documents.
marketCompare.matchesEmpty: No shared technologies or themes found between your CV and current market demand.
marketCompare.gapsEmpty: No market gaps: your CV reflects every demand theme and technology in the published pulse.
marketCompare.matchTalkingPointTemplate: Your CV aligns with current market demand for {technology}.
marketCompare.gapTalkingPointTemplate: Market demand highlights {technology}; explain adjacent experience or your learning plan.
marketCompare.gapLearningTargetTemplate: Build demonstrable {technology} experience to reflect current market demand.

console.themeLabelsDescription: Rename requirement themes for the published pulse. Overrides take effect on the next recompute and use the same publication path as the public lab view.
console.themeLabelsEmpty: No published themes yet. Start a recompute after the corpus has active documents.
console.themeLabelsError: Theme label overrides are temporarily unavailable.
console.themeLabelSavedMessage: Override saved for theme {clusterKey}. It will appear after the next recompute.
console.pulseFiltersDescription: Slice the published pulse by time, seniority, role family, and employer tier. Filters use corpus metadata and never expose raw job descriptions or compensation.
console.pulseFiltersEmpty: No filterable corpus metadata yet. Start a recompute after the corpus has active documents.

corpusWorkspace.heading: Corpus
corpusWorkspace.description: Browse and edit the job description corpus. Add listings via Intake; manage employers and review table health below.
corpusWorkspace.emptyLabel: No job descriptions yet. Parse a listing on the Intake tab to get started.
corpusWorkspace.filteredEmptyLabel: No job descriptions match the current filters.
corpusWorkspace.markdownMissingKey: This record has no storage key.
corpusWorkspace.employerUnsetWarning: No employer linked. Add one in the registry and select it here.
corpusWorkspace.employerUnknownWarning: Employer id '{id}' is not in the registry. Create it under Employers or clear the field.
corpusWorkspace.frontmatterMergedNotice: Missing frontmatter fields were filled from the database projection. Save markdown or metadata to persist them in the file.
corpusWorkspace.registryDescription: Manage employers and review counts across corpus tables.
corpusWorkspace.auditDescription: Live counts from JobDescription, Employer, Intake queue, and AnalysisRun records.
corpusWorkspace.auditErrorLabel: Could not load intake or run counts.
corpusWorkspace.employersModalDescription: Maintain employer names and tiers used by the corpus table.

employerAdmin.heading: Employer registry
employerAdmin.description: Maintain owner-defined employer names with size and prestige tiers. Employer data stays private and is never published on the public lab.

metadataAdmin.heading: Structured job description metadata
metadataAdmin.description: Link job descriptions to employers and record controlled seniority, role family, and optional compensation. Compensation and employer tiers are owner-only.

payPrestigeAnalytics.heading: Compensation and prestige analytics
payPrestigeAnalytics.description: Owner-only aggregates from structured metadata. Missing-data rates show how much of the active corpus still lacks employer links or disclosed pay.
payPrestigeAnalytics.errorLabel: Pay and prestige analytics are temporarily unavailable.
payPrestigeAnalytics.compensationEmpty: No structured compensation ranges recorded on active documents.
